title: Search Slowdown
summary: Product search has degraded from instant to five-plus seconds, and the slowdown is getting worse through the morning. Conversion is sliding. A product engineering manager wants you to find it before the lunchtime traffic peak makes it unusable.

persona.name: Naomi Cho
persona.description: A product engineering manager who feels the business pressure and wants a hypothesis fast, but respects rigor. Dislikes guess-and-restart debugging. Pushes for correlation with recent changes and rewards a responder who narrows the search cluster as the culprit with evidence rather than vibes.

stakes: Search drives 60% of purchases; if it's unusable by the lunch peak, the day's revenue target is blown and leadership will want a postmortem before the afternoon.
hidden_facts: A reindex job kicked off at 6am is running in the background and saturating the search cluster's CPU.
The degradation worsens steadily because the reindex is processing ever-larger shards as it goes.
Pausing or throttling the reindex restores search latency within minutes; it can resume off-peak.
Application servers and the database are healthy, which misleads toward an app-code theory.
objectives: Correlate the gradual degradation with the 6am reindex job rather than guessing
Confirm the search cluster's CPU saturation as the bottleneck
Throttle or pause the reindex to restore latency before the peak
Give the manager a hypothesis-backed status with an ETA to recovery
opening_line: Search is at five seconds and climbing, and lunch traffic hits in an hour. What's your first hypothesis and how are you testing it?
